segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。
贾斯珀·怀特有什么烦恼？

segments.text: Jasper White is one of those rare people who believes in ancient myths.

translations.zh-Hans: 贾斯珀·怀特是少有的相信古代神话的人之一。

segments.text: He has just bought a new house in the city, but ever since he moved in, he has had trouble with cars and their owners.

translations.zh-Hans: 他刚在城里买了一所新房子，但自从搬进去后，汽车和车主便不断给他带来麻烦。

segments.text: When he returns home at night, he always finds that someone has parked a car outside his gate.

translations.zh-Hans: 每当夜里回家，他总会发现有人把车停在他家大门外。

segments.text: Because of this, he has not been able to get his own car into his garage even once.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此，他甚至一次也没能把自己的车开进车库。

segments.text: Jasper has put up no parking signs outside his gate, but these have not had any effect.

translations.zh-Hans: 贾斯珀在大门外竖起了“禁止停车”的牌子，但没有任何效果。

segments.text: Now he has put an ugly stone head over the gate.

translations.zh-Hans: 现在他在大门上方安放了一个丑陋的石雕头像。

segments.text: It is one of the ugliest faces I have ever seen.

translations.zh-Hans: 这是我见过的最丑陋的面孔之一。

segments.text: I asked him what it was and he told me that it was Medusa, the Gorgon.

translations.zh-Hans: 我问他那是什么，他告诉我那是蛇发女怪美杜莎。

segments.text: Jasper hopes that she will turn cars and their owners to stone, but none of them has been turned to stone yet.

translations.zh-Hans: 贾斯珀希望她能把汽车和车主都变成石头，但至今还没有一个变成石头。
